title: Earthquake Magnitude Classification (PWDR)
sub_domain: Earthquake source-parameter inversion with moment-magnitude / Richter-scale categorical readout
source_data_quality_note: v2 physical_with_discrete_readout (PWDR) genesis Principle. Wraps L1-278 Earthquake Source Inversion (existing in v1 genesis with n_c=1) with deterministic magnitude-scale formulas per Hanks-Kanamori 1979 (Mw) and Richter 1935 (ML). Universal in seismology, earthquake early-warning systems (USGS ShakeAlert, JMA EEW), and tsunami warning centers. Foundational classification used by every seismic-monitoring agency globally.

gate_substitutions.conservation_substitute: softmax simplex closure on magnitude-scale ordinal readout (categorical Mw bins for warning alerts; continuous Mw for surveillance)

discrete_readout.threshold_function: Mw = (2/3) * log10(M_0) - 10.7 (Hanks-Kanamori 1979) where M_0 is seismic moment in dyne-cm; categorical readout from Mw via standard bins: micro <2.0, minor 2.0-3.9, light 4.0-4.9, moderate 5.0-5.9, strong 6.0-6.9, major 7.0-7.9, great >=8.0. Alternative scales: ML (Richter local magnitude, valid for distance < 600 km, computed from peak Wood-Anderson amplitude); mb (body-wave magnitude, computed from teleseismic P-wave); Ms (surface-wave magnitude, computed from 20-second Rayleigh wave amplitude). Tsunami warning thresholds: Pacific Tsunami Warning Center issues local watch at Mw >= 6.5 and basinwide watch at Mw >= 7.5.
discrete_readout.underlying_physical_state: earthquake source parameters (M_0 seismic moment [dyne-cm], focal mechanism, fault dimensions, slip distribution, hypocenter location) recovered by L1-278 Earthquake Source Inversion analytical core
discrete_readout.threshold_continuity_proof: Hanks-Kanamori formula Mw = (2/3) log10(M_0) - 10.7 is logarithmic continuous on M_0 > 0; categorical bins are step functions on Mw with measure-zero hyperplane boundaries at Mw = {2, 4, 5, 6, 7, 8}. Smoothed-grading globally Lipschitz with constant ~0.07 per unit M_0 uncertainty (in log10 space).

related_principles: L1-278 (Earthquake Source Inversion — analytical core, physics_core_principle)
L1-273 (Full Waveform Inversion — sibling seismic forward, informational)
L1-289 (Tsunami Propagation — sibling for cascade applications, informational)

E.description: Earthquake Magnitude Classification (PWDR): wraps L1-278 Earthquake Source Inversion analytical core with deterministic magnitude-scale formulas. Stage 1 (analytical, from L1-278): recover earthquake source parameters (seismic moment M_0, focal mechanism, fault dimensions L*W, average slip D, hypocenter location, origin time) from seismograph network observations via moment-tensor inversion or finite-fault inversion. Stage 2 (deterministic threshold): apply Hanks-Kanamori formula Mw = (2/3) * log10(M_0) - 10.7 to recover moment magnitude; classify into standard bins or compute alternative scales (ML, mb, Ms). Difficulty tier delta = 5 inherited from L1-278. Mismatch parameters: station_coverage_gap, near_field_truncation, hypocenter_localization_error, velocity_model_uncertainty, instrument_response_uncertainty, magnitude_scale_saturation.
E.forward_model: Stage 1 (L1-278): seismograph network -> moment-tensor inversion -> M_0, focal mechanism. Stage 2: Mw = (2/3) * log10(M_0) - 10.7; categorical = step(Mw at [2, 4, 5, 6, 7, 8]); tsunami_warning = step(Mw at [6.5, 7.0, 7.5]).
E.world_state_x: Per-event Mw value [Mw scale] and categorical magnitude class; for tsunami early-warning applications, per-event warning level.
E.observation_y: Multi-station seismogram waveforms (typically 10-200 stations at varying epicentral distances); broadband seismic records 0.01-50 Hz.

G.dag: L.seismograph_array -> L.waveform_acquisition -> L.moment_tensor_inversion -> L.fault_dimension_estimation -> L.magnitude_formula -> L.magnitude_threshold_classifier -> int.spatial
coupling_constraints: source_to_magnitude: seismic moment M_0 from moment-tensor inversion is multiplicatively coupled to fault dimensions and slip via M_0 = mu * A * D (rigidity * area * displacement); inherits L1-278 multi-physics coupling

W.regime: Existence inherited from L1-278. Uniqueness conditional on adequate station coverage (azimuthal gap < 180 degrees) and SNR > 10 dB. Stability dominated by velocity_model_uncertainty (~0.1-0.3 Mw bias) and magnitude_scale_saturation (ML saturates at ~7; Mw is preferred for great earthquakes). Joint Hadamard well-posedness for the source-inversion + magnitude-formula forward established by Hanks-Kanamori 1979 (foundational Mw formula), Richter 1935 (foundational ML), Kanamori 1977 (great-earthquake moment paradox), Aki-Richards 2002 (Quantitative Seismology textbook), Vallee 2013 (SCARDEC moment-tensor algorithm), Duputel et al. 2012 (W-phase moment-tensor inversion for tsunami warning).

C.solver_class: linear-operator + convex optimisation [moment-tensor inversion, finite-fault slip inversion] + categorical-readout [magnitude scale + bin] | end-to-end deep neural [DeepShakeNet, MagNet] with explicit physics-informed source-inversion regularization
C.error_bound: Inherits L1-278 source-inversion bound. Magnitude error: |Mw_hat - Mw| <= (2/3) * |log10(M_0_error / M_0)| / log(10). Misclassification probability bound: Pr(class_hat != class_true) <= C_thresh * |Mw_error| / d(Mw, bin_boundary). For Mw rounding error sigma ~ 0.1, classification error rate ~ 0.05-0.10.
C.complexity: O(N_stations * N_time_samples * N_focal_mechanism_grid) for grid-search moment tensor; faster O(N_stations * log(...)) for Bayesian inversion (e.g., SCARDEC)

recast_attempted.recast_path: Original framing: 'Classify earthquake from seismograms into magnitude bins'. Option-3 recast: 'Recover seismic moment M_0 and source parameters via moment-tensor inversion (L1-278); apply Hanks-Kanamori formula Mw = (2/3) log10(M_0) - 10.7 + magnitude bins'. Feasible because seismic source has well-defined elastic-wave forward model and magnitude scales are deterministic functions of recovered M_0.

joint_well_posedness_references: Hanks TC, Kanamori H (1979) A moment magnitude scale. J Geophys Res 84(B5):2348-2350.
Richter CF (1935) An instrumental earthquake magnitude scale. Bull Seismol Soc Am 25(1):1-32.
Kanamori H (1977) The energy release in great earthquakes. J Geophys Res 82(20):2981-2987.
Aki K, Richards PG (2002) Quantitative Seismology. 2nd ed. University Science Books.

Vallee M et al. (2011) SCARDEC: a new technique for the rapid determination of seismic moment magnitude, focal mechanism and source time functions for large earthquakes using body-wave deconvolution. Geophys J Int 184(1):338-358.
Duputel Z, Rivera L, Kanamori H, Hayes G (2012) W phase source inversion for moderate to large earthquakes (1990-2010). Geophys J Int 189(2):1125-1147.
Allen RM, Melgar D (2019) Earthquake early warning: advances, scientific challenges, and societal needs. Annu Rev Earth Planet Sci 47:361-388.

distinctness_audit.rationale: L1-278 recovers continuous source parameters (M_0, focal mechanism, fault dimensions); L1-522 wraps with magnitude-scale formula and categorical bins. Different solution_space (1D magnitude scale + bin vs continuous source-parameter ensemble); adds L.magnitude_formula and L.magnitude_threshold_classifier primitives.

v3_metadata.clinical_context: Universal use: every operational earthquake-monitoring agency globally — USGS National Earthquake Information Center, JMA Japan, EMSC Europe, GFZ Potsdam, ISC International. Earthquake early-warning systems: USGS ShakeAlert (US West Coast since 2018), JMA EEW (Japan since 2007), MyShake. Tsunami warning centers (Pacific Tsunami Warning Center, JMA, ATWC) trigger on Mw thresholds. Reference solvers: SCARDEC (Vallee), W-phase (Duputel-Rivera-Kanamori), GMT for deconvolution. PWDR formulation gives physics-grounded explainability: magnitude is derived from explicit seismic moment recovery, not black-box waveform classifier.
